_description: Complete summary of all real data available for the HOME module, extracted from command-center, process-library, and evaluaciones modules. NO mock data needed.

1_orgHomePage.description: The main org-level page.tsx that serves as the HOME view. It performs ALL data fetching server-side via Prisma and passes pre-computed props to the RiskDashboard client component.

processArchitecture.query: db.processArchitecture.findUnique({ where: { organizationId }, select: { id: true } })

processes.query: db.processDefinition.findMany({ where: { architectureId, level: 'PROCESS' }, select: { id, name, versions: [take:1], risks: [take:1] } })
processes.returns: Array<{ id: string; name: string; versions: { id: string }[]; risks: { id: string }[] }>

topRisksRaw.query: db.processRisk.findMany({ where: { processDefinition.architectureId, status NOT IN [CLOSED, ACCEPTED] }, orderBy: { riskScore: desc }, take: 5 })
topRisksRaw.returns: Array<{ id, title, description, severity, probability, riskScore, processDefinition: { name } }>

nextSession.query: db.meetingSession.findFirst({ where: { orgId, status: SCHEDULED, scheduledFor >= now }, orderBy: { scheduledFor: asc } })
nextSession.returns: { id, scheduledFor, processDefinition: { name }, status } | null

activeSession.query: db.meetingSession.findFirst({ where: { orgId, status IN [ACTIVE, CONNECTING] } })

recentSessions.query: db.meetingSession.findMany({ where: { orgId, status: ENDED }, orderBy: { updatedAt: desc }, take: 5 })
recentSessions.returns: Array<{ id, updatedAt, processDefinition: { name }, _count: { diagramNodes } }>

recentEvaluations.query: db.simulationRun.findMany({ where: { status: COMPLETED, scenario.template.organizationId }, orderBy: { completedAt: desc }, take: 5 })
recentEvaluations.returns: Array<{ id, overallScore, completedAt, scenario: { template: { title } } }>

topRisks.fields: { id, title, description, processName, severity, probability, riskScore }

allActivity.description: Merged and sorted recent sessions + evaluations, top 8

vulnerableProcesses.source: evaluacionesData.processHeatmap sorted by avgAlignment ASC, take 5

nextSteps.description: Data-driven recommendations based on process count, evaluaciones data, and vulnerable processes

returnType.overallFirst: PeriodScores { overall, alignment, riskLevel, criterio, count }

helperTypes.ProfileItem: { id, userId, overallScore, totalSimulations, strengthAreas, riskAreas, recommendedTraining, lastUpdatedAt, user: { name, email, image } }
helperTypes.ProcessProgress: { processName, processId, first: PeriodScores, latest: PeriodScores, delta, runCount }
helperTypes.MemberProgress: { userId, userName, userEmail, userImage, firstScore, latestScore, delta, totalRuns }

3_processLibraryPage.description: Server-side fetch of process list for the ProcessLibrary component
3_processLibraryPage.query: db.processDefinition.findMany({ where: { architecture.organizationId }, include: { _count: { sessions, versions, risks }, risks: [critical only] }, orderBy: { createdAt: desc } })

types.ProcessRiskSummary: { id, name, totalRisks, criticalCount, highCount, avgRiskScore, topRisk: { title, riskScore, riskType } | null }
types.OrgSummary: { totalRisks, avgScore, heatMatrix: number[][] (5x5), topRiskTypes: Array<{ type, count }> }

2_sessionsApi.returns: SessionData[] (same shape passed as initial props)

RiskDashboard.description: THE main home view component. Client-side, receives all data as props from server component.

props.nextSession: { id, scheduledFor: string, processName: string | null, status } | null

sections: Header (org name, live badge, CTA buttons)
Hero Score + Stats Row (maturity ring + 4 KPI tiles)
Quick Actions (3 cards: capture, evaluate, invite)
Evaluaciones Summary (4 cards + dimension bars + trend sparkline)
RiskDashboard.emptyState: Shows onboarding checklist when processCount === 0 && riskCount === 0

CommandCenter.description: Session management dashboard (NOT the home page, separate /descubrir route)

OrgRisksDashboard.description: Full risk management view with heat matrix, register, FMEA

types.OrgRiskData: Full risk with mitigations and controls

ProcessPhaseDashboard.description: Per-process maturity phases (contexto, captura, modelo, analisis)

exports.calculatePhaseCompleteness: returns PhaseScores { contexto, captura, modelo, analisis }

ProcessHealthRing.description: Simple SVG ring for process health score

RiskMaturityRing.description: Reusable maturity score ring with sm/md/lg sizes and color coding

hooksSummary.description: The app uses a SERVER COMPONENT pattern, not client-side hooks for data fetching. Data is fetched in page.tsx files using Prisma directly and passed as props to client components.
hooksSummary.noCustomHooks: There are NO custom useProcess, useRisks, useSessions hooks. All data is passed as props from RSC (React Server Components).

dataFlowForNewHomeModule.recommendation: The page.tsx already fetches ALL the data needed for the home view. The new HOME module components should receive the same props that RiskDashboard currently receives. You can either: (A) Refactor page.tsx to pass data to new home components, or (B) Keep the same data-fetching in page.tsx and create new sub-components that receive the data as props.
availableDataPoints: maturityScore (0-100, computed from documentation + risk coverage + evaluations)
topRisks (top 5 by riskScore, with processName, severity, probability)
evaluaciones (org avg score, dimensions, trend, completion rate, members evaluated)
